0.snippet:
Praise for Javeria Hasnain’s Sin

Every one of Hasnain’s poems is a revelation. There is an immediacy in this collection; the language dynamic yet deeply rooted in landscapes which map the sacred nece

Copyright © 2024 by Javeria Hasnain Author Photo by Stephanie Herrera Edited by James Rawlings Cover design by Maliha Mansoor All rights reserved. No part of this publication may be reproduced, distri

THE POETS

There was a time I wanted to color everything grass-green. Knew my favorite color was grass-green before I knew the color, or what favorite meant. Anything that I can put in my palm belongs

NO COUNTRY FOR YOUNG WOMEN

Lately, my poems are

becoming smaller.

My country, my

body. As if curling

into itself. A little

too early for that.

I am still young,

& I haven’t been

to Amrika yet

Acknowledgements

Grateful acknowledgements are made to the editors and readers of the journals and magazines where some of these poems first appeared, sometimes under different titles or in earlier v

1.snippet:
WATER IS A SIN

It is not the cigarettes

I am addicted to, but the smoke.

It is not the sex

I desire, but desire itself. I enjoy

the privilege of sitting

by the window when it rains,

a lit Marlb

SEX IS A SIN

I want to begin like Eve,

naked in Paradise.

Instead, I am at your feet,

hoping to feel my own

kind of rhythm. Sometimes,

I wonder what it is

like to let someone love me.

Besides,

CLOUD IN THE SHAPE OF A HUMAN

A local moazzin calls the faithful towards goodness as I step into the bathroom to masturbate. It’s (possibly) the holiest night of the holiest month, & I feel vulgar. O

HAGAR’S COMPLAINT

I wake up with You, no

matter whose bed I occupy

during the night. I mistake

You for a version of myself I

endure: call you mother in that

moth-like shade of morning.

Perhaps

BODY IS A SIN

The sin is existing.

When his half-paralyzed limbs moved across his eyes, I touched him by not looking.

I am so often touched. So often moved. And yet remain still.

Like that day,

w
